concepts.name: Decision
concepts.description: A recorded decision under decisions/YYYY/MM/DD/<id>.decision.riido.json. Conforms to decisions/schema.riido.json.

concepts.name: ConceptMap
concepts.description: This file. Captures relationships and prose constraints that DSL cannot yet express.

concepts.name: SsotDependencyMap
concepts.description: Declares which repos consume which SSOT artifacts and which generated artifacts depend on which sources. Used by tools/ssotdeps verify.

concepts.name: DevPatternEvent
concepts.description: Recorded selection of top_down vs bottom_up for a work item, written as JSONL by tools/devpattern.

concepts.name: WorkItem
concepts.description: Unit of work an agent picks up. SSOT is dsl/domain/work-item.lisp; emitted to ir/domain/work-item.ir.json; consumed by backend and frontend through generated clients (never re-declared).

concepts.name: WorkItemCreated
concepts.description: Emitted when a new WorkItem appears. SSOT is dsl/domain/work-item-created.lisp; emitted to ir/domain/work-item-created.ir.json. Backend SSE endpoint /events emits this as its only event type in decision 003's scope.

concepts.name: GeneratedClient
concepts.description: Per-language consumer-side files emitted from IR by tools/gen-go-client and tools/gen-dart-client. Committed into the consumer repo; never edited by hand; drift detected by per-repo CI gates.

concepts.name: ProbeBaseline
concepts.description: Output of `probe preflight`: the aggregate green/red state of every contracts-local verifier (decision validate, ssotdeps verify --mode local, conceptmap verify, secretscan, irdrift). status=candidate_allowed means the dumb-agent may create one candidate; status=baseline_blocked means stop.

concepts.name: EnvironmentBlocker
concepts.description: A reports/environment-blockers/<id>.md note describing an environment or setup issue (missing tool, broken baseline, missing sibling artifact). Filed by dumb-agent under a red baseline as the only allowed write. NOT a domain guard gap.

concepts.name: Fixture
concepts.description: A .json file under fixtures/positive/ or fixtures/negative/ with a .meta.json sidecar declaring fixture_type, expected (pass|fail), and optionally expected_error_contains. Walked by `probe fixtures`. v1 supports fixture_type=decision only.

relationships.example: 001-initial-agreement introduced the concept-map artifact and its schema.
Each concept/relationship/constraint optionally cites the decision that introduced it.
A decision claiming generated_artifacts must list a source whose path is declared in ssot-dependency-map.riido.json.
Creating a new WorkItem emits one WorkItemCreated event on the backend SSE surface.
backend/internal/contracts/work_item.go is the Go representation of ir/domain/work-item.ir.json; frontend/lib/contracts_client/work_item.dart is the Dart representation.
If ProbeBaseline.status is baseline_blocked, no new Fixture (positive, negative, or fuzz) may be created in that PR. Only one EnvironmentBlocker may be filed.
Every environment-blocker report cites the probe preflight output that justified filing it.

constraints.rule: No repo other than agent-cluster-contracts may define a domain vocabulary term (aggregate, entity, value-object, event, policy, lifecycle state, view).
constraints.rationale: Vocabulary is the SSOT for cross-repo communication. If backend or frontend defines its own, the same concept ends up with two names and contracts drift silently.
examples: backend writes `type WorkItem struct {...}` imported from generated code emitted by ir/domain/work-item.ir.json — OK.
frontend reads `WorkItemView` from the generated client — OK.
counterexamples: backend declares `type Task struct {...}` ad-hoc to mean what contracts calls WorkItem — NOT OK.
frontend renames `WorkItem` to `Job` in UI state — NOT OK.

constraints.rule: Every JSON IR file must declare its DSL source file path and a sha256 of that source. Mismatch fails CI.
constraints.rationale: IR is generated. Without a source pin, hand-edits are undetectable and downstream consumers lock in drift.
counterexamples: An IR file missing the `source` block.
IR sha256 not matching the current DSL file.

constraints.rule: No file committed to any of the three repos may contain a raw secret matching a known pattern (AWS key, GitHub token, JWT, PEM, etc.).
constraints.rationale: Secrets in git history are unrecoverable. Even after rotation, the trust boundary has already been crossed.
examples: Documentation states 'requires GitHub Secret named SLACK_BOT_TOKEN' — OK.
counterexamples: A fixture file containing `xoxb-...` — NOT OK.
A README pasting a `gho_...` token to show example usage — NOT OK.

constraints.rule: Every work item recorded by an agent must declare its source pattern as either top_down or bottom_up before implementation begins.
constraints.rationale: If we can't see which work was reactive vs planned, we can't later evaluate whether random selection is harmful or which kinds of bugs come from which pattern.
examples: Agent runs `tools/devpattern select --work-item WI-014` and gets `top_down`; the JSONL event is written before code changes.
counterexamples: Agent starts editing files for WI-014 with no devpattern event recorded.

guard_candidate.tool: github-action: verify devpattern event per WI in PR

constraints.rule: A dumb-agent must not create any Fixture (positive, negative, or fuzz) or domain guard-candidate report when ProbeBaseline.status is baseline_blocked. The only allowed write under a red baseline is at most one EnvironmentBlocker report.
constraints.rationale: Under a red baseline, validators are already failing on the trunk. A new failing candidate proves nothing about whether a verifier is too loose — it could just be the trunk being broken. Allowing fixture creation in this state produces noise that looks like guard gaps but isn't.
examples: probe preflight returns status=baseline_blocked. Dumb-agent files reports/environment-blockers/eb-001-sbcl-missing.md describing the broken baseline and stops. No fixture changes.
counterexamples: probe preflight returns status=baseline_blocked. Dumb-agent files a fixtures/negative/work-item/x.json claiming the verifier missed a case — the verifier did not miss anything; the baseline was simply broken.

guard_candidate.tool: probe preflight (already enforced for dumb-agent via AGENT_CONTRACT.md; future hook: pre-commit refusing fixture writes when probe preflight is red)
